title: Total RNA Extraction from Frozen Placenta Tissue
author: Scott Lindsay-Hewett
license_note: Confirmed via CrossRef DOI metadata: dx.doi.org/10.17504/protocols.io.b4b9qsr6

steps.title: Heat nuclease-free water to 95 °C
steps.instruction: Heat nuclease-free water to 95 °C .

steps.title: Pre-cool microcentrifuge to 4 °C
steps.instruction: Pre-cool microcentrifuge to 4 °C .

steps.title: Add approximately 1 mL of 1
steps.instruction: Add approximately 1 mL of 1.0mm zirconia/silica beads to each frozen placenta sample, and 700 µL mir Vana Lysis/Binding Buffer. After addition of beads and lysis buffer, tubes should be almost full. Exclude as much air as possible to reduce foaming. Samples should be in screw-cap microcentrifuge tubes with integral O-rings in the caps. Snap-cap tubes should not be used, unless secured with an adapter.

steps.title: Load samples immediately into Mini-BeadBeater-16 vial holder ring
steps.instruction: Load samples immediately into Mini-BeadBeater-16 vial holder ring. Up to 16 samples can be accommodated. IMPORTANT: Rotate the vial holder ring to a position where the small hole in the vial holder ring engages the anti-rotation pin sticking out of the wiggle mechanism. Slide the vial holder down the pin and seat it flat on the wiggle mechanism. Slide the large, black plastic hold-down cap over the stainless steel center bolt, aligning it so that it too slides down the anti-rotation pin. The hold-down cap must make contact with the top of the aluminum wiggle mechanism - not just the tops of the microcentrifuge tubes. Finally, screw on and hand-tighten the black knob firmly. Repeat: Tighten firmly.

steps.title: Switch on the Mini-BeadBeater-16 and run for 00:02:00
steps.instruction: Switch on the Mini-BeadBeater-16 and run for 00:02:00 .

steps.title: Pull frozen placenta samples (~150-200 mg...
steps.instruction: Pull frozen placenta samples (~150-200 mg each) from -80 °C and place on dry ice until ready to process. Integrity of isolated total RNA will be greater if tissue was preserved in RNA later as soon as possible after harvesting. Typically, tissue is submerged in RNA later overnight. The following day, the RNA later is removed and the sample moved to -80 °C storage.

steps.title: Add 200 proof pure ethanol to...
steps.instruction: Add 200 proof pure ethanol to mir Vana wash buffers as instructed.

steps.title: Place samples immediately into pre-chilled microcentrifuge...
steps.instruction: Place samples immediately into pre-chilled microcentrifuge and spin for 00:05:00, at maximum speed .

steps.title: Remove 500 µL lysate, being careful...
steps.instruction: Remove 500 µL lysate, being careful not to draw up particulates, and dispense into a fresh labeled microcentrifuge tube.

steps.title: Add 50 µL mir Vana miRNA...
steps.instruction: Add 50 µL mir Vana miRNA Homogenate Additive (1:10 volume of original lysate) and vortex to mix. Incubate 00:10:00 On ice .

steps.title: Add 500 µL Acid-Phenol:Chloroform (1:1 volume...
steps.instruction: Add 500 µL Acid-Phenol:Chloroform (1:1 volume of original lysate) and vortex 00:01:00 to homogenize sample. Acid-Phenol:Chloroform may appear as a clear, homogeneous phenol phase (lower), overlayed by a small aqueous phase (upper). Pipette from the lower, not the upper, phase. Phenol is very corrosive and will severely burn the skin. Safety precautions such as gloves, protective eyewear, a lab coat, and working in a fume hood are critical. Discard contaminated pipette tips in appropriate waste container.

steps.title: Spin 00:05:00, maximum speed in pre-chilled microcentrifuge
steps.instruction: Spin 00:05:00, maximum speed in pre-chilled microcentrifuge.

steps.title: Carefully remove 350 µL of the...
steps.instruction: Carefully remove 350 µL of the top aqueous phase and transfer to a fresh labeled microcentrifuge tube. It is possible to remove a greater volume to maximize RNA yield, but make sure not to disturb the interphase or organic phase. Discard phenol liquid waste and contaminated tubes in appropriate waste containers.

steps.title: Add 437
steps.instruction: Add 437.5 µL (1.25 volumes) 200 proof pure ethanol and mix thoroughly.

steps.title: Add 700 µL mir Vana Wash...
steps.instruction: Add 700 µL mir Vana Wash Solution 1 and spin 10000 x g, 4 °C, 15 seconds in pre-chilled microcentrifuge. Discard flow-through. Tip: flow-through can be aspirated into an appropriately-labeled waste container using a vacuum.

steps.title: Add 500 µL mir Vana Wash...
steps.instruction: Add 500 µL mir Vana Wash Solution 2/3 and spin 10000 x g, 4 °C, 15 seconds in pre-chilled microcentrifuge. Discard flow-through. Repeat wash step.

steps.title: After discarding flow-through from the last...
steps.instruction: After discarding flow-through from the last step, spin 10000 x g, 4 °C, 2 minutes in pre-chilled microcentrifuge to remove residual Wash Solution.

steps.title: Transfer mir Vana Filter Cartridge into...
steps.instruction: Transfer mir Vana Filter Cartridge into a fresh mir Vana Collection Tube. Add 40 µL pre-heated nuclease-free water to the center of the filter and close the cap. Incubate 00:01:00 and then spin 00:00:30, maximum speed in pre-chilled centrifuge to elute RNA.

steps.title: Transfer eluate from the mir Vana...
steps.instruction: Transfer eluate from the mir Vana Collection Tube to a low bind microcentrifuge tube and store at -80 °C . Minimize freeze/thaw cycles. Tip: Store a small aliquot in a separate tube for QC.

steps.title: Quantitate RNA using NanoDrop
steps.instruction: Quantitate RNA using NanoDrop. Pay attention to A260/A280 ratio. For highly pure RNA, a ratio of 1.8-2.1 is expected. If necessary, repurify by adding 1/10th nuclease-free 5M NaCl and 1.38 volumes 200 proof pure ethanol before repassing the sample over a fresh mir Vana Filter Cartridge. Continue the total RNA isolation from Step 17. RNA can also be quantitated using Qubit RNA Broad Range Assay.

steps.title: Transfer up to 700 µL lysate/ethanol...
steps.instruction: Transfer up to 700 µL lysate/ethanol mixture to mir Vana Filter Cartridge (placed into mir Vana Collection Tube) and spin 10000 x g, 4 °C, 15 seconds in pre-chilled microcentrifuge. Discard flow-through. Repeat with remaining volume of lysate/ethanol mixture. mir Vana Filter Cartridges can accommodate up to 700 µL volume - do not overfill. To avoid filter damage, do not spin at speeds greater than 10000 x g, °C, .

steps.title: Assess RNA quality by running the...
steps.instruction: Assess RNA quality by running the RNA 6000 Nano Assay on an Agilent 2100 Bioanalyzer. A RIN score of >7.0 is normal for total RNA isolated from placenta.

steps.title: Clean workspace, pipettes, and gloves with RNaseZAP
steps.instruction: Clean workspace, pipettes, and gloves with RNaseZAP.

steps.title: Prepare bucket of ice
steps.instruction: Prepare bucket of ice.
